statement.markdown: Implement Bubble Sort on a list of integers. However, there is a bug in the code. Identify and correct it.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a list of integers.
statement.outputFormat: Return true if the list is sorted in ascending order, false otherwise.
statement.notes: Implement the Bubble Sort algorithm without using any built-in sort functions.

examples.explanation: Input list sorted in ascending order
Input list not sorted in ascending order

hints.text: Check the Bubble Sort algorithm and find the missing swap.
Implement the swap logic to correct the Bubble Sort algorithm.

editorial.title: Editorial for Missing Swap in Bubble Sort
editorial.intuition: The Bubble Sort algorithm is missing a swap operation in the inner loop.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute-force approach is to use a nested loop to compare adjacent elements and swap them if they are in the wrong order.
editorial.betterSolution: To improve the Bubble Sort algorithm, we can add a flag to check if any swaps were made in the inner loop.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to add the missing swap operation in the inner loop and use a flag to check if any swaps were made.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that the Bubble Sort algorithm sorts the list in ascending order without using any built-in sort functions.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run to demonstrate the correct solution.
